pattern: Two numbers differ in exactly the bit positions where their XOR has a 1, so counting mismatched bits is counting the set bits of a single combined value.
hints: A bit flip changes exactly one bit from 0 to 1 or 1 to 0 -- so the minimum number of flips is exactly the number of bit positions where the two numbers disagree.
XOR-ing the two numbers produces a value with a 1 in every position where they differ and a 0 everywhere they agree.
Count the set bits in start XOR goal -- that count is the answer, with no need to flip anything explicitly.

edge_inputs.description: start is zero, goal has several bits set
goal is zero, start has several bits set
values differ only in a high bit far above the low bits
large values near the constraint maximum

stress.description: random values across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    hi = max(1, min(size, 1000000000))
    start = rng.randint(0, hi)
    goal = rng.randint(0, hi)
    return [start, goal]
